site.title: Jason Boog
site.metaDescription: The homepage for journalist Jason Boog, author of "Born Reading: Bringing Up Bookworms in a Digital Age" and "The Deep End: The Literary Scene in the Great Depression and Today."

quotes.text: Digressions, incontestably, are the sunshine — they are the life, the soul of reading!
“The reader sees the world through the detective’s eyes, experiencing the proliferation of its details as if for the first time. He has become awake to the things around him.”
quotes.attr: The New York Trilogy by Paul Auster

quotes.text: “Trust nobody and love nothing except literature."
quotes.attr: Call Me Zebra by Azareen Van der Vliet Oloomi

quotes.text: “I have not died; for I always carry a little notebook and pencil to annotate everything, and if that had happened to me, I would have written it down.”
“It’s the opposite of a blog. It’s an antiblog, because it’s meant for only one special person, and that person is you.”
quotes.attr: A Tale for the Time Being by Ruth Ozeki

quotes.text: “Whether you’re a human being, an insect, a microbe, or a stone, this verse is true. All that you touch, You Change. All that you Change, Changes you.”
quotes.attr: Parable of the Sower by Octavia E. Butler

about.headline: I am an author and journalist, and I lead the editorial team at Fable.
paragraphs: My work has appeared in The Believer, Salon, The Awl, NPR Books, The Los Angeles Review of Books, Peace Corps Writers, and other outlets. I was the west coast correspondent for Publishers Weekly.
After graduating from the University of Michigan, I spent two years teaching youth groups in Peace Corps Guatemala.

I earned my M.A. in journalism at New York University, then worked as an investigative reporter at Judicial Reports, served as the publishing editor at Mediabistro, and lead the research department at True Pictures.

resume.headline: Selected work, in reverse order.
